mcqs.question: What is the primary requirement for method overriding in Java?
mcqs.explanation: Method overriding in Java requires the same method signature, which includes the method name and parameters.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the @Override annotation in Java?

options.text: To verify that a method overrides a superclass method
To change the return type of a method

mcqs.explanation: The @Override annotation is optional but recommended, as it helps the compiler verify that a method actually overrides a superclass method.

mcqs.question: Can a final method be overridden in Java?
mcqs.explanation: A final method in Java cannot be overridden, as the final keyword prevents overriding.

mcqs.question: What is the rule for access modifiers in method overriding?

options.text: The access modifier can be more restrictive
The access modifier must be the same or less restrictive
The access modifier can only be public
The access modifier can only be private

mcqs.explanation: In method overriding, the access modifier of the subclass method cannot be more restrictive than the access modifier of the superclass method.

mcqs.question: When is the overridden method resolved in Java?
mcqs.explanation: In Java, the overridden method is resolved at runtime, which is known as runtime polymorphism.

mcqs.question: What is the benefit of method overriding in terms of code flexibility?

options.text: It makes the code more rigid
It makes the code less flexible
It allows for more flexibility in coding
It has no impact on code flexibility

mcqs.explanation: Method overriding allows subclasses to provide their own implementation of methods, making the code more flexible and reusable.

mcqs.question: How does method overriding relate to runtime polymorphism?

options.text: It is not related to runtime polymorphism
It is a type of compile-time polymorphism
It is a type of static polymorphism

mcqs.explanation: Method overriding enables runtime polymorphism, which is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms at runtime.
